title: gonocaryum macrophyllum
intro: Gonocaryum macrophyllum, also known as Indian mulberry, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, characterized by its large, edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Gonocaryum macrophyllum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent infections. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, and its leaves, fruits, and roots are used separately or in combination to harness its medicinal properties.
